name: Linux Kernel
aiCodeGenCitation: The human submitter is responsible for: * Reviewing all AI-generated code * Ensuring compliance with licensing requirements * Adding their own Signed-off-by tag to certify the DCO * Taking full responsibility for the contribution
aiCodeReviewCitation: The human submitter is responsible for: * Reviewing all AI-generated code
signOffCitation: AI agents MUST NOT add Signed-off-by tags. Only humans can legally certify the Developer Certificate of Origin (DCO).
attributionCitation: Contributions should include an Assisted-by tag in the following format::
humanReviewCitation: The human submitter is responsible for: * Reviewing all AI-generated code

name: OpenJDK
aiCodeGenCitation: Contributions in the OpenJDK Community must not include content generated, in part or in full, by large language models, diffusion models, or similar deep-learning systems.
aiCodeReviewCitation: Contributors in the OpenJDK Community may use generative AI tools privately to help comprehend, debug, and review OpenJDK code and other content, and to do research related to OpenJDK Projects, so long as they do not contribute content generated by such tools.
signOffCitation: When you create a pull request, you must check the box to affirm that your contribution is in accordance with the policy.
attributionCitation: When sharing such content, consider adding prominent comments that identify it as being AI-generated.
humanReviewCitation: In this role you are already expected to do your best to ensure that incoming contributions are consistent with OpenJDK Community policies and conventions.

name: Quarkus
aiCodeGenCitation: AI may be used to **assist your development** — for example drafting code, writing documentation, or proposing fixes — as long as **you understand, validate**, and **take responsibility for the results**.
aiCodeReviewCitation: You should only submit contributions (PRs, comments, discussions, issues) that reflect your **own understanding** and **intent**, not what an agent/LLM "spit out."
humanReviewCitation: Submitting code, tests, comments, discussions or issues that appear to be **copied directly from an AI with little or no human oversight** is **not acceptable**.

name: Fedora
aiCodeGenCitation: You *MAY* use AI assistance for contributing to Fedora, as long as you follow the principles described below.
aiCodeReviewCitation: AI tools may be used to assist human reviewers by providing analysis and suggestions.
signOffCitation: The contributor is always the author and is fully accountable for the entirety of these contributions.
attributionCitation: You *MUST* disclose the use of AI tools when the significant part of the contribution is taken from a tool without changes.
humanReviewCitation: You *MUST NOT* use AI as the sole or final arbiter in making a substantive or subjective judgment on a contribution, nor may it be used to evaluate a person’s standing within the community (e.g., for funding, leadership roles, or Code of Conduct matters).

name: GraalVM
aiCodeGenCitation: GraalVM contributors may use AI coding assistants and similar tools when preparing contributions.
aiCodeReviewCitation: For purposes of this document, "coding assistants" includes AI-based tools that help draft, transform, explain, review, or summarize code, tests, documentation, or commit text.
attributionCitation: Disclosure of AI assistance is encouraged when it helps reviewers understand how a change was produced, but explicit attribution to a specific model or tool is optional.
humanReviewCitation: The human contributor submitting a change remains responsible for the entire contribution, including any AI-assisted portion.

name: Apache Foundation
aiCodeGenCitation: Given the above, code generated in whole or in part using AI can be contributed if the contributor ensures that:
aiCodeReviewCitation: Given the above, code generated in whole or in part using AI can be contributed if the contributor ensures that:
attributionCitation: When providing contributions authored using generative AI tooling, a recommended practice is for contributors to indicate the tooling used to create the contribution.
humanReviewCitation: A contributor obtains reasonable certainty that conditions 2.2 or 2.3 are met if the AI tool itself provides sufficient information about output that may be similar to training data, or from code scanning results.

name: Python
aiCodeGenCitation: Maintainers may close issues and PRs that are not useful or productive, including those that are fully generated by AI.
aiCodeReviewCitation: Authors must review the work done by AI tooling in detail to ensure it actually makes sense before proposing it as a PR.
humanReviewCitation: Authors must review the work done by AI tooling in detail to ensure it actually makes sense before proposing it as a PR.

name: Firefox
aiCodeGenCitation: For contributors who choose to use these tools, we trust them to do so with care, just as we trust them with every other part of Firefox's development.
aiCodeReviewCitation: The role of the [reviewer](./Code_Review_FAQ.rst) is to double-check the work of a human, not the output of a tool.
humanReviewCitation: You're expected to understand and be able to explain every change you make.
